content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
P.O. Box 807 • -J.2R J Street. Sacramento. CA l)5S12-0~O; 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 32:!>(l886 
Mark Bryant 
Post Office Box 1368 
Redway, California 95660 
Re: Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 1-99-007 
Dear Mr. Bryant: 
March 10, 1999 
You requested written advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political 
Reform Act (the "Act'V You are a board member and vice chairman of the Redway 
Community Services District ("RCSD"). You are also a general engineering contractor. You 
were hired by Bill Sheppard, a contractor, to assist with a project that he was doing for the 
RCSD. You inquired as to whether it posed a conflict for you to work for Mr. Sheppard. As a 
board member, you were not involved in the decision to hire the contractor to do the project. 
During our telephone conversation on February 10, 1999, we discussed the conflict-of-
interest provisions, specifically focusing on source of income issues. I informed you that I am 
unable to respond to your written request because it involves an issue of past conduct and we can 
only render advice on prospective matters. (Regulation 18329 (b )(8)(A).) 
You sent a follow-up letter regarding your participation in board decisions and actions 
regarding Mr. Sheppard and his payment for services rendered to the district. You may not 
participate in any decision in which Mr. Sheppard is directly involved since he is a source of 
income to you of over $250 or more in the past twelve months. (Section 87103(c), Regulations 
18700,18704.1 and 18705.3, copies enc1osed.)2 You also may not participate in any decision in 
which Mr. Sheppard is indirectly involved if there will be a reasonably foreseeable material 
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109-
18995, of the California Code of Regulations. Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity 
provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).) 
2 Please note that the conflict-of-interest regulations were numerically reorganized in November 1998. 
There were no substantive changes to the law. 


File No. 1-99-007 
Page 2 
financial effect on Mr. Sheppard. (Regulation 18705.3.) Without more facts regarding a specific 
decision, it is only possible to give you this general advice. 
1 am enclosing several advice letters which deal with source of income issues. 1 hope 
these will be of assistance to you for general guidance. 
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at 
(916) 322-5660. 
SGC:JS:t\s 
Enclosures: 
Savidge Advice Letter, No. 1-94-049 
Tunnel Advice Letter, No. 1-88-123 
Huguet Advice Letter, No. 1-87-330 
Harron Advice Letter, No. 1-86-027 
Regulation 18700 
Regulation 18704.1 
Regulation 18705.3 
Sincerely, 
Steven G. Churchwell 
General Counsel 
By: 
~ 
.. 
. 
. tt/UA) 
~~her 
Sfaff Counsel, Legal Division

sections.question: You are a board member and vice chairman of the Redway Community Services District (RCSD). You are also a general engineering contractor. You were hired by Bill Sheppard, a contractor, to assist with a project that he was doing for the RCSD. You inquired as to whether it posed a conflict for you to work for Mr. Sheppard.
sections.conclusion: You may not participate in any decision in which Mr. Sheppard is directly involved since he is a source of income to you of over $250 or more in the past twelve months. You also may not participate in any decision in which Mr. Sheppard is indirectly involved if there will be a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on Mr. Sheppard.
sections.question_synthetic: Can a district board member who is employed by a contractor work for that contractor on a project being performed for the district, and what restrictions apply to the board member's participation in decisions involving the contractor?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The board member cannot participate in any decisions directly involving the contractor who is a source of income exceeding $250, nor in decisions indirectly involving the contractor if there would be a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on that contractor.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is informal advice letter with clear question and conclusion sections. Letter indicates initial oral advice on February 10, 1999 regarding past conduct (which FPPC could not address), followed by prospective advice on the follow-up inquiry regarding future board participation. File number 1-99-007 matches header format.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You are a board member and vice chairman of the Redway Community Services District (RCSD). You are also a general engineering contractor. You were hired by Bill Sheppard, a contractor, to assist with a project that he was doing for the RCSD. You inquired as to whether it posed a conflict for you to work for Mr. Sheppard.

CONCLUSION: You may not participate in any decision in which Mr. Sheppard is directly involved since he is a source of income to you of over $250 or more in the past twelve months. You also may not participate in any decision in which Mr. Sheppard is indirectly involved if there will be a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on Mr. Sheppard.

embedding.summary: FPPC advised a Redway Community Services District board member regarding conflict-of-interest restrictions when working as a contractor for another contractor performing work for the district. The FPPC concluded the board member must recuse from decisions involving the contractor as a source of income.
